attributes.description: Alerts when the oldest unprocessed message on a queue exceeds a configurable age threshold. This is the headline SQS processing-lag signal.
attributes.name: [AWS SQS OTel] Oldest message age high

investigation_guide.blob:
## Oldest Message Age High

`ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage` measures how long the oldest visible message has waited. A sustained climb means consumers are not keeping pace or a poison message is stuck.

### Triage Steps
1. Check consumer fleet health — are workers running, scaled, and healthy?
2. Compare `NumberOfMessagesDeleted` vs `NumberOfMessagesSent` for the affected queue.
3. Inspect `ApproximateNumberOfMessagesVisible` (backlog) and `ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible` (in-flight).
4. Look for poison messages — if no DLQ is configured, failed messages loop until retention expires.
5. Verify visibility timeout is appropriate for your processing duration.

### Customisation
- Adjust the age threshold in the final `WHERE` clause — 300 seconds suits real-time queues; batch pipelines may need hours.
- CloudWatch approximate metrics can lag by up to ~15 minutes.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.sqs.otel-*
// Exclude DLQs — DLQ depth is covered by a separate alert
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/SQS"
    AND MetricName == "ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage"
    AND stat == "Maximum"
    AND QueueName NOT LIKE "*dlq*"
| STATS max_age_seconds = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/SQS/ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage`)
    BY QueueName, cloud.region
// Tune to your SLA: 300s = 5 minutes (real-time); batch queues may need hours
| WHERE max_age_seconds > 300
